publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
publicationstmt.aspace_export_error:
ASPACE EXPORT ERROR : YOU HAVE A PROBLEM WITH YOUR EXPORT OF YOUR RESOURCE. THE FOLLOWING INFORMATION MAY HELP:
Java::JavaSql::SQLSyntaxErrorException: Table 'archivesspace_prod.accession' doesn't exist ["com.mysql.cj.jdbc.exceptions.SQLError.createSQLException(com/mysql/cj/jdbc/exceptions/SQLError.java:120)", profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-23 18:16:21 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Notebooks of architectural class notes and drawings; student papers; notebooks and sketchbooks on gardens; photographs of gardens and parks; architectural drawings and renderings; pen and ink drawings; and articles on architectural topics.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Charles Downing Lay attended the School of Architecture at Columbia University from 1896 to 1900 and received a S.B. in Landscape Architecture from Harvard University's School of Landscape Architecture in 1902. From 1902 to 1948 he maintained a landscape architecture practice in New York City. He served as editor and publisher of Landscape Architecture with H. V. Hubbard and Robert Wheelwright. His projects included town planning for Albany, N.Y. and for the United States Housing Corporation in Erie and Butler, Pa.; parks in New York City, Troy, Albany, Schenectady, and Brooklyn, N.Y., Washington, D.C., and Stratford, Connecticut; school grounds in New York, Massachusetts, and Connecticut; subdivisions in New York, Massachusetts, Rhode Island, Connecticut, and New Jersey; and housing in New York City. He also was consulting architect to the 1939 New York World's Fair and worked on conservation efforts in the Housatonic River valley, founding the Housatonic Valley Planning Association in 1948.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Notebooks of architectural class notes and drawings, 1898, 1899; student papers; notebooks and sketchbooks on gardens; photographs of gardens and parks, especially Marine Park in Brooklyn; architectural drawings and renderings; pen and ink drawings; and articles on architectural topics. Also, papers relating to lectures; and club memberships, including the Century Club; miscellaneous correspondence; and condolence letters upon his death.

odd.head: PUBLIC AREAS INCLUDED IN THE COLLECTION

list.item: Cemetary of the Gate of Heaven, Mount Pleasant, NY
Central Park (A. Mitchell Memorial) New York City
(Old) Essex County Golf Course, East Orange, New Jersey
North End Junior High School, Stratford, Connecticut
Recreation Terraces and Public Baths, New York City
St. Nicholas Houses, Manhattan, New York
Sheepshead Bay Houses, Brooklyn, New York City
Stratford, Connecticut War Memorial and other
Verona Civic Center, Verona, New Jersey

odd.head: PRIVATE ESTATES INCLUDED IN THE COLLECTION

list.item: Elbridge L. Adams, Great Barrington, Massachusetts
George B. Agnew, Lewisboro, New York
Robert C. Anderson, Upper Montclair, New Jersey
J. Charles Andrews, Great Neck, Long Island
J. Percy Bartram, Caritas Island, Stamford, Connecticut
Mrs. Edgar W. Bassick, Fairfield, Connecticut
A. Bijur, Long Branch, New Jersey
Mrs. H. S. Bowen/Belton Farms, Round Hill, Greenwich, Connecticut
Thomas Bowen and J. Moss Ives, Mattapan, Locust Valley, Long Island
A. P. Budd, Brewster, New York
Anson W. Burchard, Locust Valley, Long Island
J. P. Chamberlain, Waterbury and Middlebury, Connecticut
K. F. Cooper, Great Neck, Long Island
Don W. Murray Crane, Dalton, Massachusetts
W. M. Crane, Jr., Dalton, Massachusetts
Mrs. Z. M. Crane, Dalton, Massachusetts
John W. Davis, Locust Valley, Long Island
Mrs. W. B. Devreux, Jr., Syosset, Long Island
W. A. Donald, South Yarmouth, Massachusetts
A. A. Fowler, Peapack, New Jersey
S. Miller Gallaher, Charleston, West Virginia
Miss Grata Hostetter, Locust Valley, Long Island
Mr. & Mrs. Jerome C. Hunsaker, Brookville, Long Island
J. Percy Keating, St. Martins, Pennsylvania
S. Forry Laucks, York County, Pennsylvania
H. H. Lehman, Harrison, New York
Mr. and Mrs. Edward Mogul, Easton, Connecticut
Mr. & Mrs. Winchester Noyes, Huntington, Long Island
G. Pagenstecher, Storm King, New York
Andrew J. Post, Sound Beach, Old Greenwich, Connecticut
Pratt Mausoleum, Glen Cove, Long Island
William A. Siewers, White Plains, New York
R. H. Simpson, Oyster Bay, New York
L. A. Cappelan Smith, ___ Point, Long Island
George J. Stockley, Great Neck, Long Island
Mrs. Lyman Beecher Stowe, Forest Hills Gardens, Long Island
T. L. Van Norden, South Salem, New York
Mr. & Mrs. Alexander Van Rensselaer, Fort Washington, Pennsylvania
Mrs. Willis D. Wood, Huntington, Long Island
William Zinsser, Great Neck, Long Island

relatedmaterial.head: RELATED MATERIALS
relatedmaterial.p: Related collections: Oliver Ingraham Lay, Charles Downing Lay, and Lay family papers at the Archives of American Art, https://www.aaa.si.edu/collections/oliver-ingraham-lay-charles-downing-lay-and-lay-family-papers-6011.

arrangement.head: SERIES LIST
arrangement.p: Series I. Personal Box 1, Folders 1-4; Box 18, Folder 1; Box 19
Series II. Student Papers Box 1, Folders 5-14
Series III. Correspondence Box 1, Folders 15-39; Box 18, Folder 5-6
Series IV. Art and Artists Box 1, Folders 40-56
Series V. Private Homes and Estates Box 1, Folders 57-68
Series VI. Architecture, Planning, etc.: Housing, Parks, Committees and Other Box 2, Folders 1-49
Series VII. Writings Box 2, Folder 50 - Box 3, Folder 22; Box 18, Folder 10
Series IX. Photographs and negatives of private homes, estates, public areas and other Boxes 8-17
Series X. Oversize blueprints, sketches, drawings, paintings and photographs Box 19; Mapcase Folder 1-13

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Charles Downing Lay papers, #4477. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.subject: Architects and housing developers.
Landscape architects.
Gardens -- Design.
Housing -- New York (N.Y.)
Housing development -- New Jersey.
Housing development -- Connecticut.
Housing development -- Rhode Island.
Housing development -- New York (State)
School grounds -- Connecticut -- Stratford.
School grounds -- Washington (D.C.)
School grounds -- New York (State)
City planning -- Pennsylvania -- Butler.
City planning -- Pennsylvania -- Erie.
City planning -- New York (N.Y.)
Landscape architecture.
controlaccess.title: Landscape architecture.
controlaccess.corpname: New York World\'s Fair (Location of meeting: New York, N.Y.). Date of meeting or treaty signing: (1939-1940 :.)
